title: TheravńĀda self-label: vńĀda-sense collocation/frame profile across strata

hypotheses.h1: H1 (house thesis: canon supple, commentary systematizes). The self-designation cluster (theravńĀda / theriya / vibhajjavńĀda / tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiya / mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsin) is essentially ABSENT from the mula canon as a SCHOOL label and appears almost entirely in the commentarial layers (attha + tika), with measurable per-Mchar density rising from ~0 in 1early/2late/3abh to a non-trivial commentarial ceiling in 5comm/6tika. CRUCIALLY, the dominant syntactic frame of theravńĀda-/vńĀda- tokens is NOT a self-conscious sectarian school-name contrastively coordinated with rival nikńĀya names, but the Gethin frame: vńĀda functions as a near-synonym of nikńĀya/ńĀcariyavńĀda, heading a 'thera's vńĀda' / elder-lineage equation (theriyavńĀda, theravńĀda = theriyńĀnaŠĻÉ vńĀdo), i.e. an internal doctrinal-position / teachers'-opinion sense, with the rival-name-contrast frame confined to a small subset concentrated in the KathńĀvatthu-aŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠hakathńĀ (Kv-a). The commentary systematizes a LINEAGE-of-elders identity, not a crystallized sectarian self vs other.
hypotheses.h0: H0 (pre-committed null, reported if it wins). Either (a) DENSITY NULL: the self-label cluster shows no monotone canon‚Üícommentary density rise distinguishable from generic register/length artifacts (the matched negative-control register terms move the same way), OR (b) FRAME NULL: among coded vńĀda-frame rows the rival-nikńĀya-contrast frame is NOT a small Kv-a-concentrated subset but is broadly distributed and is the plurality frame in commentary ‚ÄĒ i.e. theravńĀda already reads as a contrastive sectarian school-name across the commentarial corpus, not predominantly as an elder-lineage/teachers'-opinion equation. Under H0(b) the Gethin 'vńĀda‚ČąnikńĀya, not a separate school' reading is NOT the corpus-dominant frame.

frozen_lexica.name: theravada_core
frozen_lexica.notes: Covers theravńĀda + cases (-o/-aŠĻÉ/-ena/-assa/-e/-ńĀ) via truncated stem theravńĀd, plus agentive theravńĀdin/theravńĀdńę/theravńĀdino/theravńĀdissa/theravńĀdńĀnaŠĻÉ. \m anchors the left boundary; compound-internal occurrence after a fusing/geminating leading member is caught by theravada_sandhi below.

frozen_lexica.name: theravada_sandhi
frozen_lexica.role: sandhi/gemination + periphrastic-genitive variants of theravńĀda that \m on the bare stem would miss
frozen_lexica.notes: ttheravńĀd catches gemination after a vowel-final preceding member. therńĀnaŠĻÉ/therassa + vńĀd catches the periphrastic 'thera's/elders' vńĀda' genitive equation (the Gethin frame written out, not compounded). Includes both anusvńĀra spellings ŠĻÉ (SC) and ŠĻĀ (some CST renderings).

frozen_lexica.name: theriya
frozen_lexica.role: elders'-tradition adjective / alternate self-label (theriya, theriyńĀ, theriyavńĀda, theriyanikńĀya)
frozen_lexica.notes: theriya/theriyńĀ/theriye/theriyńĀnaŠĻÉ + compounds theriyavńĀda, theriyanikńĀya all begin theriy. High-precision single stem: theriy- is not a prefix of unrelated common words. This is the term Gethin says the Kv-a 'school' sense should be equated with (theriyavńĀda).

frozen_lexica.name: vibhajjavada
frozen_lexica.notes: vibhajjavńĀd is the Pali (geminate -jj-) CST/SC form; vibhajavńĀd guards a degeminated variant; vibhajyavńĀd guards the Sanskritized -jy- spelling in case any dictionary_entries lemma carries it. The Sanskrit form is NOT expected in mula/attha Pali but a zero there is itself informative, so include it explicitly rather than silently miss it.

frozen_lexica.name: tambapanniya
frozen_lexica.notes: tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiya/-ka are the school-name variants; tambapaŠĻáŠĻáńę is the island/personal form; tambapaŠĻáŠĻáidńępa (island compound) guarded because the school name derives from the place. Retroflex ŠĻáŠĻá is load-bearing: a dental-n spelling is a wrong-diacritic trap covered by tambapanniya_dental_guard.

frozen_lexica.name: tambapanniya_dental_guard
frozen_lexica.role: dental-n mis-spelling safety net for tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiya (must not silently zero)
frozen_lexica.notes: Pure ingest-defect safety: fires only if an ingest normalized ŠĻá‚Üín or dropped a ŠĻá. Expected to return 0 in a correctly-diacriticized corpus; a non-zero here flags an ingest normalization issue, NOT a finding.

frozen_lexica.name: mahaviharavasin
frozen_lexica.notes: -vńĀsin declines vńĀsńę (nom sg) / vńĀsino (gen,pl) / vńĀsinaŠĻÉ etc. Stem mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi covers vńĀsńę after the long-ńę shortens before some endings; both ńĀ in mahńĀ- and -vihńĀra- are mandatory long vowels. A short-a mis-spelling (mahavihar...) is the wrong-diacritic trap, guarded by mahaviharavasin_short_guard.

frozen_lexica.name: mahaviharavasin_short_guard
frozen_lexica.role: short-a mis-spelling safety net for mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsin (must not silently zero)
frozen_lexica.notes: Pure ingest-defect safety: fires only if an ingest stripped the long ńĀ (mahńĀ‚Üímaha / vihńĀra‚Üívihara). Expected 0 in a correctly-diacriticized corpus; non-zero flags an ingest normalization issue, NOT a finding.

frozen_lexica.name: vada_frame_anchors
frozen_lexica.role: FRAME lexicon ‚ÄĒ equation/coordination heads that disambiguate vńĀda-sense (vńĀda as nikńĀya,kula,ńĀcariyavńĀda)
frozen_lexica.notes: Used ONLY in co-occurrence windows around a self-label hit to classify the frame: ńĀcariyavńĀda (teachers' doctrine = vńĀda), nikńĀya (the term Gethin says vńĀda substitutes for), kula/kulavńĀda (lineage-family), samayantara/laddhi (doctrinal-position vocab). For CODING/collocation, not for density.

frozen_lexica.name: rival_nikaya_names
frozen_lexica.role: NEGATIVE/CONTRAST CONTROL ‚ÄĒ real rival school names expected to co-occur contrastively if a sectarian frame exists
frozen_lexica.notes: Andhaka/UttarńĀpathaka/Pubbaseliya/Aparaseliya are the rival positions actually NAMED and refuted in the KathńĀvatthu(-aŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠hakathńĀ) ‚ÄĒ so this control is the POSITIVE test for the contrastive-school frame. mahiŠĻÉsńĀsaka has ŠĻÉ,ńę,dental-s variants; mahńĀsaŠĻÖghika has ŠĻÖ vs ŠĻÉ nasal variants ‚ÄĒ both guarded. If theravńĀda were a crystallized sectarian self-name we expect contrastive co-occurrence with THESE in the same passage; matched on register (all -ka/-vńĀda school-name morphology in the same commentarial texts).

frozen_lexica.name: thera_alone_control
frozen_lexica.role: NEGATIVE CONTROL ‚ÄĒ thera/therńĀ alone (elder, not the -vńĀda compound) to bound generic 'elder' register
frozen_lexica.notes: thera alone is ubiquitous and high-density across ALL strata (every named monk-elder). It must NOT show the canon‚Üícommentary self-label spike. If thera-alone density tracks theravńĀda density, the 'crystallization' signal is just generic elder-talk register. \M (non-word-boundary's complement = end-boundary) after each case-ending excludes the theravńĀd-/theriy- stems (matched by their own lexica).

enumeration_sql.label: 00_layer_denominators_sanity
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Confirm per-layer Mchar denominators against live corpus (dedup via is_primary). If these drift from the committed constants (mula 53.543921 / attha 29.488533 / tika 28.357746) STOP and re-derive before computing densities.
SELECT stratum(work_slug) AS strat,
       CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'CANON'
            WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'ATTHA'
            WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'TIKA'
            ELSE 'OTHER' END AS layer,
       count(*) AS rows,
       sum(char_length(original))/1000000.0 AS mchar
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary
GROUP BY 1,2 ORDER BY 1,2;

enumeration_sql.label: 01_selflabel_density_per_stratum_per_mchar
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- PRIMARY density pass: per-stratum per-Mchar density + raw N for each self-label lexicon (sparse => exhaustive census, no sig test).
WITH base AS (
  SELECT stratum(work_slug) AS strat, original, char_length(original) AS cl
  FROM passages WHERE is_primary
)
SELECT strat,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theravńĀdin|theravńĀdńę|theravńĀdino|theravńĀdiss|theravńĀdńĀn)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_theravada,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '(ttheravńĀd|therńĀnaŠĻÉ\s+vńĀd|therassa\s+vńĀd)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_theravada_periphrastic,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theriy)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_theriya,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(vibhajjavńĀd|vibhajavńĀd|vibhajyavńĀd)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_vibhajjavada,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiy|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáik|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáńę|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáidńęp)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_tambapanniya,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsńę|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsin|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsino)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_mahaviharavasin,
       sum(cl)/1000000.0 AS mchar,
       round( sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theravńĀdin|theravńĀdńę|theravńĀdino|theravńĀdiss|theravńĀdńĀn)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / (sum(cl)/1000000.0), 3) AS dens_theravada_per_mchar,
       round( sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theriy)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) / (sum(cl)/1000000.0), 3) AS dens_theriya_per_mchar
FROM base GROUP BY strat ORDER BY strat;

enumeration_sql.label: 02_selflabel_density_by_layer_role
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- Density on the CANON/ATTHA/TIKA layer partition (the H1 axis: ~0 in canon, ceiling in commentary). pli-vism shelved as commentary per convention.
WITH base AS (
  SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'CANON'
              WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'ATTHA'
              WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'TIKA' ELSE 'OTHER' END AS layer,
         original, char_length(original) AS cl
  FROM passages WHERE is_primary
)
SELECT layer,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theravńĀdin|theravńĀdńę|theravńĀdino|theravńĀdiss|theravńĀdńĀn)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_theravada,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theriy)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_theriya,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(vibhajjavńĀd|vibhajavńĀd|vibhajyavńĀd)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_vibhajjavada,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiy|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáik|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáńę)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_tambapanniya,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsńę|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsin|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsino)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_mahaviharavasin,
       sum(cl)/1000000.0 AS mchar,
       round(sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theravńĀdin|theravńĀdńę|theravńĀdino|theravńĀdiss|theravńĀdńĀn)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/(sum(cl)/1000000.0),3) AS dens_theravada,
       round(sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(theriy)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/(sum(cl)/1000000.0),3) AS dens_theriya
FROM base GROUP BY layer ORDER BY layer;

enumeration_sql.label: 03_negative_control_density
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- NEGATIVE CONTROLS density per layer: thera-alone (generic elder register; must be HIGH everywhere and NOT spike like a self-label) + rival-nikaya-names (must be near-0 outside Kv-a/commentary). Plus ingest-defect guards (must be 0).
WITH base AS (
  SELECT CASE WHEN work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism' THEN 'CANON'
              WHEN (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism') THEN 'ATTHA'
              WHEN work_slug LIKE '%-tika' THEN 'TIKA' ELSE 'OTHER' END AS layer,
         original, char_length(original) AS cl
  FROM passages WHERE is_primary
)
SELECT layer,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(ther[ao]\M|therńĀ\M|therassa\M|therńĀnaŠĻÉ\M|therena\M|theraŠĻÉ\M)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_thera_alone,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(mahiŠĻÉsńĀsak|mahisńĀsak|sabbatthivńĀd|sabbatthik|vajjiputtak|andhak|uttarńĀpathak|pubbaseliy|aparaseliy|mahńĀsaŠĻÖghik|mahńĀsaŠĻÉghik|gokulik)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_rival_names,
       sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(tambapanniy|tambapannik|mahavihar)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS n_ingest_defect_guard_should_be_0,
       sum(cl)/1000000.0 AS mchar,
       round(sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(ther[ao]\M|therńĀ\M|therassa\M|therńĀnaŠĻÉ\M|therena\M|theraŠĻÉ\M)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/(sum(cl)/1000000.0),3) AS dens_thera_alone,
       round(sum(CASE WHEN original ~* '\m(mahiŠĻÉsńĀsak|mahisńĀsak|sabbatthivńĀd|sabbatthik|vajjiputtak|andhak|uttarńĀpathak|pubbaseliy|aparaseliy|mahńĀsaŠĻÖghik|mahńĀsaŠĻÉghik|gokulik)' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/(sum(cl)/1000000.0),3) AS dens_rival_names
FROM base GROUP BY layer ORDER BY layer;

enumeration_sql.label: 04_cooccurrence_selflabel_x_rival
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- CO-OCCURRENCE pass (contrastive-school-frame test): passages where a self-label AND a rival nikaya name appear TOGETHER. H1 predicts FEW and concentrated in Kv-a (kv-attha). Reported per work_slug so the concentration is visible.
SELECT work_slug, stratum(work_slug) AS strat, count(*) AS n_cooccur
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary
  AND original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theriy|vibhajjavńĀd|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiy|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi)'
  AND original ~* '\m(mahiŠĻÉsńĀsak|mahisńĀsak|sabbatthivńĀd|sabbatthik|vajjiputtak|andhak|uttarńĀpathak|pubbaseliy|aparaseliy|mahńĀsaŠĻÖghik|mahńĀsaŠĻÉghik|gokulik)'
GROUP BY work_slug ORDER BY n_cooccur DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: 05_cooccurrence_selflabel_x_vada_frame_anchors
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- FRAME pass (Gethin equation test): self-label co-occurring with vada/nikaya/acariyavada/kula equation heads in the SAME passage. H1 predicts this DOMINATES vs the rival-name co-occurrence.
SELECT work_slug, stratum(work_slug) AS strat, count(*) AS n_vada_frame
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary
  AND original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theriy|vibhajjavńĀd|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiy|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi)'
  AND original ~* '\m(ńĀcariyavńĀd|nikńĀy|kulavńĀd|kul[ańĀ]\M|ekavńĀd|a√Ī√ĪavńĀd|samayantar|laddhi)'
GROUP BY work_slug ORDER BY n_vada_frame DESC;

enumeration_sql.label: 06_rows_to_code_selflabel_hits
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- ROWS TO BE CODED (blind sense+frame audit, the %carita% lesson): every is_primary passage containing a self-label token, with id+slug+role+snippet, ordered for sampling. Coders tag SENSE {school|elder-lineage/teachers-opinion|unclear} and FRAME {coordinated-with-rival|vada/kula/acariyavada-equation|genitive thera's-vada|bare/other}. If N>400, sample stratified by layer (seed-fixed).
SELECT id, work_slug, work_role, stratum(work_slug) AS strat,
       char_length(original) AS cl,
       substring(original FROM 1 FOR 600) AS snippet
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary
  AND original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theravńĀdin|theravńĀdńę|theravńĀdino|theravńĀdiss|theravńĀdńĀn|theriy|vibhajjavńĀd|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiy|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáik|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsńę|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsin|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsino)'
ORDER BY strat, work_slug, id;

enumeration_sql.label: 07_gethin_reproduction_sense_split_kva
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- METHODS-VALIDATION (NOT a finding): reproduce Gethin's claim that the school-contrast sense concentrates in Kathavatthu-atthakatha. Pull ALL self-label hits from kv-attha with fuller context for the reproduction sense-coding check against his published count.
SELECT id, work_slug, work_role,
       substring(original FROM 1 FOR 1200) AS context
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary
  AND work_slug ILIKE 'kv%' AND work_slug LIKE '%-attha'
  AND original ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theriy|vibhajjavńĀd|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiy|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi)'
ORDER BY id;

enumeration_sql.label: 08_lexicographic_axis_dictionary
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- DPD/DPPN proper-name vs PED/DPD common-noun lexicographic axis: pull dictionary headword treatments of the self-labels to classify each as proper-name (DPPN) vs common-noun (PED/DPD). Read-only context for the lexicographic-axis section.
SELECT source, lemma, substring(definition FROM 1 FOR 500) AS def_snippet
FROM dictionary_entries
WHERE lemma ~* '\m(theravńĀd|theriy|vibhajjavńĀd|tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiy|mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsi|theravńĀdin)'
ORDER BY source, lemma;

enumeration_sql.label: 09_thera_alone_codingframe_guard
enumeration_sql.sql:
-- NEGATIVE-CONTROL row sample: thera-alone hits in commentary that do NOT contain any self-label stem, mixed into the coding set as a discrimination/foil calibration set (őļ guard). Reproducible sample via id modulo.
SELECT id, work_slug, stratum(work_slug) AS strat, substring(original FROM 1 FOR 400) AS snippet
FROM passages
WHERE is_primary
  AND (work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism' OR work_slug LIKE '%-tika')
  AND original ~* '\m(ther[ao]\M|therńĀ\M|therassa\M|therńĀnaŠĻÉ\M)'
  AND original !~* '\m(theravńĀd|theriy)'
  AND (id % 97) = 0
ORDER BY id;

density_method: Per-character, never raw rows (T2). For each lexicon, density = (count of is_primary passages whose original matches the diacritic-correct regex) / (Mchar of that stratum-or-layer), where Mchar = sum(char_length(original))/1e6 over the same is_primary partition. Two partitions reported: (1) stratum() 7-way; (2) the CANON/ATTHA/TIKA layer partition using CANON = work_role='mula' AND work_slug<>'pli-vism'; ATTHA = work_slug LIKE '%-attha' OR work_slug='pli-vism'; TIKA = work_slug LIKE '%-tika'. Committed denominators (mula 53.543921, attha 29.488533, tika 28.357746 Mchar) are sanity-checked by query 00 before any density is trusted; if live sums drift materially, re-derive and re-freeze before reporting. Because the cluster is SPARSE this is an exhaustive census: report raw N alongside every density, run NO significance test, and never compare raw row counts across layers (only per-Mchar). Co-occurrence/frame passes (04/05) are reported as raw N per work_slug (structural, not density) since the question there is concentration (which works), not magnitude. stratum()/layer is a work_slug LOOKUP (T1): a canon‚Üícommentary density rise is presented as a usage-ceiling pattern in the commentarial register, NOT as independent chronological dating evidence.
negative_controls: thera-alone (ther[ao]/therńĀ/therassa/therńĀnaŠĻÉ... WITHOUT the -vńĀda or theriy- stem): the generic 'elder' register term. High-density across ALL layers including canon. It MUST NOT show the canon‚Üícommentary self-label spike. If theravńĀda density and thera-alone density rise together by the same factor, the apparent crystallization is a generic elder-talk register/length artifact ‚ÄĒ H0(a) wins.

rival-nikńĀya-names matched on register (mahiŠĻÉsńĀsaka/sabbatthivńĀda/vajjiputtaka/andhaka/uttarńĀpathaka/pubbaseliya/aparaseliya/mahńĀsaŠĻÖghika/gokulika): same -ka/-vńĀda school-name morphology, same commentarial texts. Doubles as the POSITIVE test for the contrastive-school frame ‚ÄĒ if theravńĀda were a crystallized self-name, query 04 should show it co-occurring contrastively with THESE broadly (not just in Kv-a). Their near-absence outside kv-attha bounds the contrastive frame.

ingest-defect guards (tambapanniy/tambapannik dental-n, mahavihar short-a): expected 0 in a correctly-diacriticized corpus. A non-zero return flags an ingest normalization defect (ŠĻá‚Üín, ńĀ‚Üía), NOT a finding ‚ÄĒ protects against the wrong-diacritic silent-zero trap operating in the other direction (it confirms the diacritic-correct regexes aren't missing normalized text).
falsifiers: H1 density falsified if theravńĀda/theriya self-label density in CANON (work_role='mula', excl pli-vism) is NOT near-zero ‚ÄĒ i.e. if the mula canon already carries the self-label at a per-Mchar density comparable to the commentarial layers.
H1 density falsified if the negative-control thera-alone term shows the SAME canon‚Üícommentary multiplicative rise as the self-label cluster (signal is generic elder register, not school-naming).

H1 frame falsified if, among blind-coded self-label rows, the rival-nikńĀya-CONTRAST frame is the plurality frame across commentary generally (not a small Kv-a-concentrated subset) ‚ÄĒ i.e. theravńĀda predominantly reads as a sectarian school-name coordinated against rivals rather than as a vńĀda‚ČąnikńĀya/elder-lineage equation (= H0(b)).
H1 frame falsified if query 04 (self-label √ó rival-name co-occurrence) is NOT concentrated in kv-attha but spread roughly proportionally to layer size across all commentaries.

Gethin reproduction (methods-validation only) fails if the kv-attha sense-split count in query 07 cannot be brought within tolerance of his published school/elder/unclear count under blind coding ‚ÄĒ this invalidates the coding instrument, not the H1/H0 corpus claim.
predicted_signal: PREDICTED (not yet measured): CANON self-label density ‚Čą 0 for theravńĀda/vibhajjavńĀda/tambapaŠĻáŠĻáiya/mahńĀvihńĀravńĀsin (post-canonical names); theriya may show a trace in 2late/3abh. ATTHA + TIKA carry essentially the entire cluster, with a non-trivial per-Mchar ceiling concentrated in vinaya/Kv commentaries and chronicle-adjacent works. PREDICTED frame split among coded rows: the vńĀda‚ČąnikńĀya / ńĀcariyavńĀda / elder-lineage-equation frame is the PLURALITY (Gethin); the rival-name-contrast frame is a MINORITY concentrated in kv-attha. PREDICTED negative controls: thera-alone HIGH and roughly flat-to-rising across all layers (no self-label-shaped spike); rival-names near-0 outside kv-attha; ingest guards = 0. TO BE MEASURED (no prediction committed): exact per-Mchar magnitudes, absolute size of the kv-attha contrastive subset, the DPD/DPPN-vs-PED proper/common split, and whether tika adds anything beyond attha. The Gethin kv-attha reproduction count is a TO-BE-MEASURED methods-validation target, explicitly NOT a finding.
scope_limits: Cannot DATE the crystallization of 'TheravńĀda' as a sectarian identity. The corpus shows only the commentarial-CEILING usage; the modern/sectarian sense's emergence is an out-of-corpus question (Pali epigraphy, 18th-c. inscriptions, colonial-era usage) this DB cannot touch.
stratum()/layer is a work_slug LOOKUP, not independent chronology (T1): a canon‚Üícommentary gradient is a register/text-layer pattern, not dated evidence of when the term arose.

No foreign-language text: Sanskrit TńĀmraŇõńĀŠĻ≠ńęya/VibhajyavńĀda and Chinese/parallel attestations are NOT in the corpus (passage_parallels is graph-only, parallel_have=false for non-pli). Cross-traditional naming is out of scope; structural-graph mentions only.

Cannot settle the DOCTRINAL question of whether the MahńĀvihńĀra 'was' a self-conscious school ‚ÄĒ only the corpus-linguistic frame profile of the LABELS. Findings are register/density/collocation, never a verdict on sectarian self-consciousness.

CST coarse-vagga vs SC fine-sutta ingest means 'distinct works/suttas carrying the label' counts are edition-sensitive; per-Mchar density (edition-robust) is the headline and distinct-work counts are is_primary-deduped and approximate.
Sparse-census design: no inferential statistics; a layer with 0‚Äď3 hits is reported as raw N, not extrapolated.
iaa_plan: Blind double-coding with Cohen's őļ on rows from query 06 (all self-label hits; if N>400, a layer-stratified seed-fixed random sample). Two independent coders (or one human + one agent, blind to each other and to stratum/layer) apply TWO codebooks per row: (1) SENSE ‚ąą {school-as-separate-entity, elder-lineage/teachers'-opinion (vńĀda‚ČąnikńĀya/ńĀcariyavńĀda), unclear}; (2) FRAME ‚ąą {coordinated/contrasted-with-named-rival, vńĀda/kula/ńĀcariyavńĀda-EQUATION, genitive thera's-vńĀda, bare/other}. Snippets shown context-only with work_slug/role MASKED. őļ computed separately for SENSE and FRAME. Pre-committed thresholds: őļ‚Č•0.67 report frame proportions; 0.4‚Č§őļ<0.67 add an adjudication round (third coder) and re-report; őļ<0.4 report raw counts only and flag the instrument. Query 09 thera-alone rows are mixed in as a discrimination/foil calibration set. The Gethin kv-attha reproduction (query 07) is coded under the same blind protocol and reported as methods-validation, separate from the H1/H0 finding.
primary_loci: KathńĀvatthu-aŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠hakathńĀ (kv-attha) ‚ÄĒ Gethin's locus where the school-vs-elder sense, if anywhere, surfaces as theriyavńĀda; refutations of Andhaka/UttarńĀpathaka/Pubbaseliya/Aparaseliya positions
SamantapńĀsńĀdikńĀ / Vinaya-aŠĻ≠ŠĻ≠hakathńĀ (vinaya commentary) ‚ÄĒ Gethin's other focus; the single-lineage-of-Theras (ńĀcariyavńĀda) framing
